[182:58]After the 2020 election, I was furious because at that time, I believed, and I still do believe

[183:04]that Donald Trump was cheated out of that election.

[183:07]And I was furious that we let it happen because we knew it was coming.

[183:13]I did a show with Alex Jones in June, I believe, of 2020.

[183:20]that they would count up the votes on election day, it would appear that Trump was winning.

[183:25]And then once they started counting the mail-in ballots, of which there would be many because

[183:29]of the pandemic, over time, Joe Biden would take the lead and would be declared the president,

[183:36]and they would steal the election from Trump.

[183:39]I was furious, not only that the election was stolen,

[183:43]when we knew precisely how it would happen,

[183:46]the manner in which it would happen.

[183:48]But in particular, I was furious at the Republicans

[183:51]because they abandoned Trump.

[183:54]Trump's legal counsel in the White House abandoned him.

[183:58]They made no legal case that the election was stolen.

[184:01]They gave up.

[184:02]The Republicans in the House and the Senate mounted no serious effort to stop the steal

[184:07]of the election.

[184:08]Neither did the Republicans in the state capitals.

[184:28]is that there were two Senate runoff elections in Georgia,

[184:31]where the elections were too close to call

[184:33]or something like that.

[185:03]who, in my view, were extremely corrupt and represented the epitome of the Republican establishment.

[187:25]So I was building up my non-profit, America First Foundation. I was building up my political

[187:31]infrastructure. We were trying to enter politics. In February, 2021, Representative Paul Gosar

[188:51]I got in touch with Joe Kent through his campaign consultant, Matt Brainerd.

[189:08]I was introduced to him by Michelle Malkin, she was friends with him, he was very involved

[189:17]I had a phone call with Joe Kent.

[189:19]I believe in the summer of 2021.

[189:22]And the phone call went like this.

[189:24]I called up Joe Kent, which by the way, he does not deny.

[189:27]And I have text messages with him.

[189:30]I said, listen up, Joe.

[189:32]We want to support you in this election.

[189:35]I said, we want to help you build up your social media.

[189:38]We want to retweet your profile.

[189:41]We want the groipers to support you.

[189:42]We want a doorknock for you.

[189:44]We want a fundraise for you.

[189:47]I said, but here's the caveat.

[189:49]I said, and you can take this or leave it.

[189:51]I said, but we want to help you.

[189:54]We want to get you into office.

[189:56]I was very pro-Jo Kent.

[189:58]I said, but here's the deal.

[190:00]You can't disavow me.

[190:02]I said, now, if you want our support, it's contingent on that.

[190:06]I said, because although I'd like to keep this discreet,

[190:09]and we don't have to tell everybody that we're helping you,

[190:12]I said, but if the day ever comes

[190:14]that the left asked you about me for any reason,

[190:17]I said, you cannot disavow.

[190:19]I said, because if we start disavowing each other,

[190:24]this is how the right eats itself,

[190:26]and this is how the left wins.

[190:27]I said, today you're disavowing me,

[190:29]tomorrow somebody's disavowing you.

[190:32]What's more, I said, I don't want to help people.

[190:34]I barely have an operation.

[190:36]I don't want to use what little I have to help somebody if they're going to turn around

[190:39]and throw me under the bus.

[190:41]Because that's exactly what happened with Paul Gosar during AFPAC 2.

[190:50]And there were other events too, but that was the big one.

[190:59]He said, and you know what?

[191:01]He said, the Democrats are the real racist.

[191:29]We were supporting his social media.

[216:49]It's a Nazi idea.
